title: Zoological Survey Officer
description: Zoological Survey Officers conduct research on animal diversity, distribution, and conservation. They work in the field and laboratory, collecting data, analyzing specimens, and contributing to wildlife management and policy.
content: Zoological Survey Officers play a crucial role in understanding and protecting India's rich biodiversity. They conduct surveys, identify species, assess conservation status, and contribute to policy recommendations for wildlife protection. This career requires a strong background in zoology, ecology, and conservation biology, along with excellent analytical and communication skills.

financialFeasibility.note: Higher education is essential, but scholarships and educational loans are available. Government jobs offer stability and decent salaries, making it accessible to middle-class families.

sectorScore.remarks: The Zoological Survey of India (ZSI) and other government organizations are primary employers. Opportunities in NGOs and private conservation organizations are also growing.

relatedCareers.reason: Studies wildlife populations and their habitats to develop conservation strategies.
relatedCareers.title: Wildlife Biologist

relatedCareers.reason: Assesses environmental impacts and develops solutions for environmental problems.
relatedCareers.title: Environmental Scientist

relatedCareers.reason: Manages and protects forest resources and wildlife within forest areas.
relatedCareers.title: Forest Officer

genderDistribution.note: Increasing female participation in zoology and conservation is gradually balancing the gender distribution.
